Patch Set 3:

Because this failed on a send, I think this has to do with the crude attempt to force non-primary NID which likely lacks protection rather than with the code it is aiming to test

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-2

Same stack/LBUG on several other tests.  I didn't find "match" in any of the console logs I looked at, so I don't think the patch is triggering the new code because all of the testing is done with only one interface. 

Mark this -2 so it doesn't land, but we need to find some way to exercise the code. Could we configure a second LNet interface on the clients by patching test-framework.sh somehow?

Patch Set 3:

Serguei, are you able to trigger the lnet_try_match_md() code/CWARN() in your local testing with this patch?

Patch Set 3:

No I haven't been able to so far. I need to modify my test setup to make sure nodes have multiple NIDs.

Patch Set 3:

This patch can probably be abandoned?

Abandoned

Replaced with 53872

parents.subject: LU-17476 lnet: prefer to use bits only to match ME

commit.subject: LU-17476 lnet: force non-primary NID in LNetPut
commit.message:
LU-17476 lnet: force non-primary NID in LNetPut

This is a debug patch which forces use of non-primary
target NID in LNetPut attempting to simulate the scenario
when this may occur unintentionally and thus test the
related changes to MD matching code on the other side
of the connection

Test-Parameters: fortestonly trivial testlist=sanity-lnet
Signed-off-by: Serguei Smirnov <ssmirnov@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: I4df58f1f0eb41613a482e062a15db7f0395a7e13
